solution.rawText:
This requires us to first differentiate the function. We therefore write
                                        1вҲ’x         2     1
                                     y= вҲҡ3
                                               = xвҲ’ 3 вҲ’ x 3
                                           x 2

which we can differentiate to get
                                      dy           5         2
                                         = вҲ’ 32 xвҲ’ 3 вҲ’ 13 xвҲ’ 3
                                      dx
This is zero when
                                             5        2
                                      вҲ’ 32 xвҲ’ 3 вҲ’ 31 xвҲ’ 3 = 0,

so multiplying by 3x 3 to clear fractions in both the coefficients and in the powers gives
                     5




                                           вҲ’2 вҲ’ x = 0

so x = вҲ’2. (We could also have obtained this by writing вҲ’ 23 xвҲ’ 3 = 13 xвҲ’ 3 and dividing one side
                                                                   5         2


by the other.)
We next need to determine the sign of dx    dy
                                               in each region. We note that the function is not
defined at x = 0, so we have to deal with x < 0 and x > 0 separately. It is also not clear how
to find the sign of dx
                    dy
                       directly from its given form, so we first factorise it, giving

                                      dy           5
                                         = вҲ’ 31 xвҲ’ 3 (2 + x).
                                      dx
(Incidentally, this gives yet another way to see that the derivative is zero at x = вҲ’2.) We can
now work out the signs of the two factors, and hence of the derivative, in the various ranges:

                                 x < вҲ’2    x = вҲ’2     вҲ’2 < x < 0       x>0
                          вҲ’ 35
                         x         вҲ’         вҲ’            вҲ’             +
                         2+x       вҲ’          0           +             +
                          dy
                          dx       +          0           вҲ’             +

It is therefore increasing in the region x < вҲ’2 and x > 0. (It it not increasing at x = вҲ’2, but
rather it is stationary at that point.) The correct answer is therefore A (though the question
mistakenly says x в©Ҫ вҲ’2).
